dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (2% width, 20-period MA) as volatility bands around a 20-period moving average. DJTA reference proxy mapped to close price comparisons. Entry long: price within normal bands (above lower band). Entry short: price below lower band (extreme deviation). Exits are reversals at band boundaries. Position size multiplier (1.5×) noted but not encoded in rules structure—apply at execution layer. The 'DJTA as proxy for S&P 500' relationship is noted but both are treated as price series in this rule engine.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (2% width, 20-period MA) as volatility bands around a 20-period moving average. DJTA reference proxy mapped to close price comparisons. Entry long: price within normal bands (above lower band). Entry short: price below lower band (extreme deviation). Exits are reversals at band boundaries. Position size multiplier (1.5×) noted but not encoded in rules structure—apply at execution layer. The 'DJTA as proxy for S&P 500' relationship is noted but both are treated as price series in this rule engine.

source_rule_text: Use a reference index (proxy) with volatility bands to generate directional signals in a correlated target market: trade long when the proxy is within normal bands, and trade short only during rare extreme deviations below the lower band.
